name: python-snappy

metadata.name: python-snappy
metadata.summary: Python library for the snappy compression library from Google

documentation:
Python bindings for the snappy compression library from Google.

More details about Snappy library: http://google.github.io/snappy

modules.name: snappy.snappy_formats
documentation:
Consts and function to handle target format.
ALL_SUPPORTED_FORMATS - list of supported formats
get_decompress_function - returns stream decompress function for a current
    format (specified or autodetected)
get_compress_function - returns compress function for a current format
    (specified or default)

functions.name: snappy.snappy_formats.uvarint
documentation: Read uint64 nbumber from varint encoding in a stream

functions.name: snappy.snappy_formats.check_unframed_format
documentation:
Can this be read using the raw codec

This function wil return True for all snappy raw streams, but
True does not mean that we can necessarily decode the stream.

functions.name: snappy.snappy_formats.guess_format_by_header
documentation:
Tries to guess a compression format for the given input file by it's
header.

```
return

format name (str), stream decompress function (callable)
```

functions.name: snappy.__main__.cmdline_main
documentation:
This method is what is run when invoking snappy via the commandline.
Try python -m snappy --help

modules.name: snappy.snappy
documentation:
python-snappy

Python library for the snappy compression library from Google.
Expected usage like:

    import snappy

    compressed = snappy.compress("some data")
    assert "some data" == snappy.uncompress(compressed)
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: snappy.snappy.stream_compress
documentation:
Takes an incoming file-like object and an outgoing file-like object,
reads data from src, compresses it, and writes it to dst. 'src' should
support the read method, and 'dst' should support the write method.

The default blocksize is good for almost every scenario.

functions.name: snappy.snappy.stream_decompress
documentation:
Takes an incoming file-like object and an outgoing file-like object,
reads data from src, decompresses it, and writes it to dst. 'src' should
support the read method, and 'dst' should support the write method.

The default blocksize is good for almost every scenario.
\:param decompressor_cls: class that implements `decompress` method like

> StreamDecompressor in the module

```
param start_chunk

start block of data that have already been read from
the input stream (to detect the format, for example)
```

classes.name: snappy.snappy.StreamCompressor

methods.name: snappy.snappy.StreamCompressor.add_chunk
documentation:
Add a chunk, returning a string that is framed and compressed. 

Outputs a single snappy chunk; if it is the very start of the stream,
will also contain the stream header chunk.

methods.name: snappy.snappy.StreamCompressor.copy
documentation: This method exists for compatibility with the zlib compressobj.

This class implements the compressor-side of the proposed Snappy framing
format, found at

    http://code.google.com/p/snappy/source/browse/trunk/framing_format.txt
        ?spec=svn68&r=71

This class matches the interface found for the zlib module's compression
objects (see zlib.compressobj), but also provides some additions, such as
the snappy framing format's ability to intersperse uncompressed data.

Keep in mind that this compressor object does no buffering for you to
appropriately size chunks. Every call to StreamCompressor.compress results
in a unique call to the underlying snappy compression method.

classes.name: snappy.snappy.StreamDecompressor

methods.name: snappy.snappy.StreamDecompressor.check_format
documentation:
Does this stream start with a stream header block?

True indicates that the stream can likely be decoded using this class.

methods.name: snappy.snappy.StreamDecompressor.decompress
documentation:
Decompress 'data', returning a string containing the uncompressed
data corresponding to at least part of the data in string. This data
should be concatenated to the output produced by any preceding calls to
the decompress() method. Some of the input data may be preserved in
internal buffers for later processing.

This class implements the decompressor-side of the proposed Snappy
framing format, found at

    http://code.google.com/p/snappy/source/browse/trunk/framing_format.txt
        ?spec=svn68&r=71

This class matches a subset of the interface found for the zlib module's
decompression objects (see zlib.decompressobj). Specifically, it currently
implements the decompress method without the max_length option, the flush
method without the length option, and the copy method.

methods.name: snappy.snappy.HadoopStreamCompressor.add_chunk
documentation:
Add a chunk, returning a string that is framed and compressed. 

Outputs a single snappy chunk; if it is the very start of the stream,
will also contain the stream header chunk.

methods.name: snappy.snappy.HadoopStreamCompressor.copy
documentation: This method exists for compatibility with the zlib compressobj.

methods.name: snappy.snappy.HadoopStreamDecompressor.check_format
documentation: Does this look like a hadoop snappy stream?

methods.name: snappy.snappy.HadoopStreamDecompressor.decompress
documentation:
Decompress 'data', returning a string containing the uncompressed
data corresponding to at least part of the data in string. This data
should be concatenated to the output produced by any preceding calls to
the decompress() method. Some of the input data may be preserved in
internal buffers for later processing.
